brands.name: Neighborly (Mr. Rooter)
brands.notes: Mr. Rooter Plumbing is a 50+ year-old plumbing/drain-cleaning franchise and a Neighborly brand. It is a large, stable home-services system (229 franchised + 3 company units as of 12/31/2024) with a robust Item 19 disclosure ($2.0M average / $1.27M median AUV). Figures sourced to the 2025 FDD via Franchise Chatter.

riskFlags.title: Total fee burden at upper end of home-services norm
riskFlags.detail: Ongoing fees total roughly 8% of gross sales: 6% royalty plus a 2% national marketing (MAP) fee, with up to an additional 3% Local Marketing Group contribution. This is typical-to-slightly-high for the trades-services category but not an outlier.

franchiseFee.note: $42,500 base plus $425 per 1,000 population over the territory minimum

royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross sales, with minimums and special roll-in rates

adFundPercent.note: 2% national MAP fee; up to an additional 3% local marketing group contribution

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19 (2025 FDD, full-year 2024): average annual gross sales $2,009,644 across 188 franchises; median $1,270,728. Represents total gross sales from plumbing/drain services, not profit.

yearFounded.note: CONFLICT: most sources cite founded 1970; a minority cite 1968

item19Available.note: Discloses average and median AUV plus distribution

veteranDiscount.note: VetFran member: 20% off the initial franchise fee for honorably discharged veterans

brands.name: QC Kinetix
brands.notes: QC Kinetix is a regenerative-medicine / non-surgical pain franchise that underwent sharp contraction in 2024 (184 to 167 units, systemwide sales -28.6%) and faces consumer class-action litigation plus at least one franchisee arbitration alleging misrepresentation. New CEO (June 2024) launched a 'QCK 2.0' turnaround. High-risk profile.

riskFlags.title: Sharp unit contraction and systemwide sales decline
riskFlags.detail: QC Kinetix began 2024 with 184 units and ended with 167 (net loss of 17). Systemwide sales fell 28.6%, from $157M (2023) to $112M (2024). CEO Mark Montini disclosed that ~10 units were 'technically open but never actually started operating' and another 30-40 had ceased operations by mid-2024. Roughly half the closures were 'strategic' market exits; the rest were franchisees who could not sustain operations financially.

riskFlags.title: Consumer class-action litigation over deceptive practices
riskFlags.detail: Class actions allege QC Kinetix marketed unproven stem-cell/regenerative treatments as 'scientifically validated and FDA-approved' when they are neither, omitted FDA-mandated warnings, used high-pressure electronic-signature sales, and advertised '0% interest / low monthly payment' financing while enrolling patients in loans at 9.99%+ APR. Includes Robertson v. QC Franchise Group LLC et al. (filed Nov. 2023, settled/dismissed with prejudice) and related FDUTPA actions.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee arbitration alleging misrepresentation at sale
riskFlags.detail: A franchisee (Stephen Alexander) brought an arbitration alleging the franchisor made numerous misrepresentations to induce purchase of two franchises; the arbitration was stayed pending mediation. One franchisee also noted litigation created reputational challenges with financing partners.

riskFlags.title: Leadership churn during downturn
riskFlags.detail: Mark Montini replaced Scott Hoots as CEO in June 2024, mid-contraction, and launched a 'QCK 2.0' recovery strategy. Q2 2025 reportedly showed the first same-clinic sales growth in five quarters, but the turnaround is recent and unproven.

riskFlags.title: High upfront capital and fee load
riskFlags.detail: Initial investment runs roughly $250,100-$655,080 with a $55,000 franchise fee, 8% royalty, 1% brand-development fee, plus heavy mandated local marketing spend ($20,000+/month in early months) and up to $1,800/month technology fee. High burn relative to revenue contributed to franchisee failures.

totalInvestmentMin.note: CONFLICT: Entrepreneur (current) lists $250,100-$655,080; FDD-derived sources (franchisepayback/franchiseoverview) cite $227,330-$495,860

royaltyPercent.note: 8% of weekly gross revenue, with monthly minimums ($1,000 months 3-5, $1,500 from month 6)

adFundPercent.note: 1% brand-development fee; separately, large mandated local marketing spend (~$20,000+/month early on)

totalUnits.note: 167 at year-end 2024 (down from 184 at start of 2024). Entrepreneur's 2026 listing shows 133, indicating continued contraction.

veteranDiscount.note: $1,000 off initial franchise fee for honorably discharged veterans; not applicable to additional franchises

isGrowing.note: Entrepreneur shows -3% unit growth; system contracted from ~184 to 133 units across 2024-2026

brands.name: Rolling Suds
brands.notes: Rolling Suds is a power/soft-washing franchise; the original business dates to ~1990 (Warrington, PA, founder Brian Wendling Sr.) but the franchise launched in 2022/2023. Fast-growing (64 franchised units by end of 2024) with a modest Item 19 (5 mature units averaged $419,193 in 2024). Early-stage system; limited operating history.

riskFlags.title: Very young franchise with thin Item 19 base
riskFlags.detail: The franchise only launched in 2022/2023 (7 units opened in 2023). The 2024 Item 19 average of $419,193 is based on just 5 franchised outlets open the entire calendar year, a small sample that may not represent newer or scaled units (range $206,608-$670,883).

riskFlags.title: Conflicting cost/fee figures across sources
riskFlags.detail: Investment ranges and franchise-fee figures vary widely across aggregators ($136K-$499K range cited; fee reported as both $54,900 and $75,000). The 2025 FDD (per Franchise Chatter) shows $186,150-$251,850 total investment and a $54,900 franchise fee.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Range $186,150-$251,850 per 2025 FDD; other aggregators cite wider ranges

franchiseFee.note: CONFLICT: 2025 FDD per Franchise Chatter shows $54,900; PeerSense lists $75,000

royaltyPercent.note: Greater of 8% of gross revenue or applicable minimum royalty

adFundPercent.note: 2% brand fund; plus local advertising requirement of greater of 4% of gross revenue or $2,500 per service vehicle/month

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD Item 19 (2025 FDD, full-year 2024): average gross revenue $419,193 across only 5 franchised outlets open the full year; median $384,467; range $206,608-$670,883. Gross revenue, not profit.

yearFounded.note: Original power-washing business founded ~1990 in Warrington, PA

yearFranchising.note: CONFLICT: sources cite franchising began 2022; FDD shows first franchised units opened in 2023

isGrowing.note: Rapid expansion from 7 units (2023) to 64 franchised (end 2024)

brands.name: Prime IV Hydration & Wellness
brands.notes: Prime IV Hydration & Wellness is a fast-growing IV-therapy/wellness franchise (founded 2017-2018 in Denver, CO; began franchising 2020). Entrepreneur's current listing shows 215 units. Limited public Item 19 detail (franchisor discloses less financial performance than category peers). Conflicting royalty figures (7% vs 8%) across sources.

riskFlags.title: Limited financial-performance transparency
riskFlags.detail: Multiple aggregators note Prime IV discloses substantially less Item 19 financial-performance information than other franchises in the IV/wellness category, making unit-economics due diligence harder for prospective franchisees.

riskFlags.title: Regulatory exposure inherent to IV-therapy model
riskFlags.detail: IV hydration/wellness clinics operate in a medically regulated space (medical-director requirements, scope-of-practice and state board rules for IV administration vary by state). This is a category-wide regulatory consideration rather than a Prime-IV-specific finding; no Prime-IV-specific lawsuit was located in this research.

riskFlags.title: Conflicting fee figures across sources
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is reported as both 7% and 8%, marketing/ad fee as 1% to 2%, and total investment ranges differ widely by source ($164K-$632K). Entrepreneur's current listing shows 7% royalty, 1%-2% ad fee, and $187,701-$631,193 investment.

totalInvestmentMin.note: CONFLICT: Entrepreneur lists $187,701-$631,193; FDD-derived sources cite ranges as low as $164,633 and as high as $632,000

franchiseFee.note: Some sources cite a lower-tier fee starting around $34,000

royaltyPercent.note: CONFLICT: Entrepreneur lists 7%; several FDD-derived aggregators cite 8%

adFundPercent.note: Reported as 1%-2% (Entrepreneur shows 1%-2%; some sources cite 1%)

totalUnits.note: 215 units per Entrepreneur 2026 listing; marketing materials cite 300+ open or in development

yearFounded.note: CONFLICT: Entrepreneur lists 2017; some sources cite 2018 (Denver, CO; founder Amy Neary)

isGrowing.note: Named one of North America's fastest-growing franchises (IFA, 2024)

brands.name: Red Barn Homebuyers
brands.notes: Red Barn Homebuyers is a real-estate-investing / house-flipping franchise founded by HGTV personalities Ken and Anita Corsini; the franchise launched in 2022. Low entry cost ($59K-$258K) but a high 10% royalty. Entrepreneur shows strong unit growth (54 units in 2024 FDD to ~150 in 2026). Note royalty-structure conflict (10% vs a flat per-year fee per Entrepreneur).

riskFlags.title: High royalty relative to category and ongoing-fee conflict
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived sources report a 10% royalty, high for the model. Entrepreneur's listing instead shows an ongoing fee of roughly '$6K+/yr' (a flat/minimum structure) with $0 ad royalty, which conflicts with the 10% figure. Prospective franchisees should confirm the exact royalty basis against the current FDD.

riskFlags.title: Real-estate wholesaling/flipping model carries market and regulatory risk
riskFlags.detail: House-flipping/real-estate-investment franchises are exposed to housing-market cycles, interest-rate sensitivity, and tightening state rules around wholesaling and marketing of equitable interests. This is a category/model risk; no Red-Barn-specific lawsuit was located in this research.

riskFlags.title: Conflicting franchise-fee figures
riskFlags.detail: Franchise fee is reported as both $29,500 and $34,500 across sources; Entrepreneur's current listing shows $34,500. Founding-year data also conflicts (operator history from 2005 vs. franchise entity organized 2021, franchising 2022).

franchiseFee.note: CONFLICT: Entrepreneur shows $34,500; some sources cite $29,500

royaltyPercent.note: CONFLICT: FDD-derived sources report 10%; Entrepreneur lists an ongoing fee of '~$6K+/yr' (flat/minimum) with $0 ad royalty. Verify royalty basis against current FDD.

totalUnits.note: 54 franchised per 2024 FDD; Entrepreneur's 2026 listing shows ~150 units and marketing cites 185+ franchisees

yearFounded.note: CONFLICT: franchise entity organized Aug 2021; Entrepreneur lists 2005 (the Corsinis' operator/flipping history start)

isGrowing.note: Strong unit growth (54 units in 2024 FDD to ~150 in 2026 listing); #1 in category on Entrepreneur Franchise 500 (2026)
